title: Regarding ABCD method for QCD estimation

posts.name: Sanskar Nanda
posts.text:
Hello everyone,
I am currently working on the tt̄ semi-leptonic electron+jets channel and I am applying the ABCD method for QCD estimation.
For defining the regions, I have used the cut-based electron ID and mT as my two variables.My region definitions are as follows:
A: cutBased = Veto, mT < 40
B: cutBased = Tight, mT < 40
C: cutBased = Veto, mT > 50
D: cutBased = Tight, mT > 50 (signal region)
As a first check, I estimated the QCD yield in each region using:
N_i=N_data−N_nonQCDMC, i∈{A,B,C,D}
When I computed the ratios, I found:
NA/NC=0.909
NB/ND=0.48
So as you can see the ratios are not even close. So, this had led me to rethink my variable choices. Has anyone encountered a similar issue? Could you kindly advise me on which variables are generally considered good choices for defining the ABCD regions for analysis involving electrons?
I would be very grateful for any advice or insights on this.
Best Regards,
Sanskar Nanda
